subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Boards
- **1 main game board** — Central board with action spaces for commissioning, absolving, attacking, fortifying, and converting. Three attribute tracks: Faith, Influence, and Strength.
- **4 player boards** — Personal boards with worker holding area, tavern, garrison, and workshop slots.

#### Workers
- **5 worker colors** — Blue, green, red, purple, and gold workers. Each color affects your attribute tracks when placed.
- **Neutral workers** — Black workers available from certain actions.
- **Outsiders** — Dark red meeples representing enemy raiders.

#### Cards
- **Paladin cards** — 12 unique paladins, each providing a special power. One is active per round.
- **Townsfolk cards** — Recruited townspeople providing ongoing or one-time abilities.
- **King's Order cards** — End-game scoring objectives.
- **King's Favor cards** — Bonus cards earned through attribute advancement.

#### Other Components
- **Provisions, silver, workers** — Standard resources.
- **Fortification tiles** — Placed on the city wall for defense VP.
- **Outsider tokens** — Raiders threatening the city.
- **Debt cards** — Negative effects taken when resources are tight.
- **Virtue/debt track** — Tracks moral standing.

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Central Board
1. **Place the main board** — All action spaces and attribute tracks visible.
2. **Worker supply** — Sort workers by color into supply pools on the board.
3. **Outsiders** — Place outsider tokens on designated raid spaces.
4. **Fortification tiles** — Stack fortification tiles by type.
5. **Townsfolk deck** — Shuffle and deal a display of townsfolk cards.
6. **King's Orders** — Deal end-game scoring cards to each player.

#### Round Setup
- **7 rounds** — Place round markers.
- **Paladin deck** — Shuffle paladins. Each round, players will draft one.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Starting Resources
- **Player board** — Take a personal board.
- **Starting workers** — Receive a starting allocation of workers in various colors.
- **Starting resources** — Small amount of provisions and silver.
- **King's Orders** — Receive secret end-game scoring objective cards.
- **Virtue track** — Start at the neutral position.

#### Attribute Tracks
- **Faith** — Starts at base. Advances through religious actions.
- **Influence** — Starts at base. Advances through political actions.
- **Strength** — Starts at base. Advances through military actions.
- **Track bonuses** — Passing threshold markers on attribute tracks grants bonuses.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content:
#### Core Concept
Paladins of the West Kingdom is a worker-placement game about defending a city on the western border of the Frankish kingdom. Players recruit workers of different colors, which affect three key attributes (Faith, Strength, Influence) when placed. You commission new buildings, absolve debts, attack invading outsiders, and fortify the city walls. Each round, a unique paladin provides a special ability, and managing the virtue/debt system is critical.

#### Game Arc
- **7 rounds** — Each round, a new paladin is selected and workers are placed.
- **Attribute advancement** — Faith, Influence, and Strength unlock increasingly powerful rewards.
- **Worker color matters** — Placing different colored workers adjusts your attributes.
- **Virtue vs. debt** — Virtuous actions vs. morally questionable shortcuts create a tension throughout.
- **No central blocking** — Unlike many worker-placement games, the main restriction is your personal worker supply, not shared spaces.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Structure
1. **Paladin selection** — Reveal paladins and draft/assign one to each player. Each paladin has a unique ability active for this round.
2. **Place workers** — Players take turns placing workers from their personal supply onto action spaces on the main board or their player board.
3. **Resolve actions** — Each placement immediately triggers the action.
4. **Round end** — Return the paladin; prepare for next round.

#### Worker Placement Rules
- **Color requirements** — Each action space requires workers of a specific color or combination.
- **Attribute shifts** — When you place a colored worker, your attributes shift:
  - **Blue** — Increases Faith.
  - **Red** — Increases Strength.
  - **Green** — Increases Influence.
  - **Purple** — Versatile but may affect multiple attributes.
  - **Gold** — Wild color; can substitute for any.
- **Worker acquisition** — Recruit workers from the supply during the game. The color you recruit shapes your strategy.

#### Actions Available
- **Commission** — Build a townsfolk card, gaining its ability and VP.
- **Absolve** — Remove debt cards from your hand for VP and benefits.
- **Attack** — Fight outsider raiders for VP and Strength advancement.
- **Fortify** — Add tiles to the city wall for VP and defensive bonuses.
- **Convert** — Advance on the Faith track and gain religious bonuses.
- **Garrison** — Place workers in your garrison for end-game scoring.
- **Recruit** — Add new workers from the supply to your personal pool.
- **Pray/Tax/Conscript** — Faction-aligned actions on your player board.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Commissioning
- **Build townsfolk** — Pay the cost on a townsfolk card (workers + resources) to add it to your tableau.
- **Ongoing abilities** — Some townsfolk provide permanent effects.
- **VP value** — Each townsfolk is worth VP at game end.
- **Synergies** — Townsfolk can combo with your paladin abilities and attribute levels.

#### Attacking Outsiders
- **Raids** — Outsiders appear on the board each round, threatening the city.
- **Combat** — Spend workers and Strength to defeat outsiders.
- **Rewards** — Defeating outsiders earns VP, resources, and Strength advancement.
- **City defense** — Leaving outsiders unchecked may cause penalties.

#### Fortifying
- **Wall tiles** — Place tiles on the city wall track.
- **Cost** — Resources and workers.
- **VP** — Each tile is worth VP, with bonuses for consecutive placement.
- **Strength bonus** — Fortifying also advances Strength.

#### Absolving Debt
- **Debt cards** — Accumulated through aggressive or costly actions.
- **Absolution** — Spend Faith and workers to remove debt cards.
- **Virtue** — Removing debt advances your virtue track.
- **Penalty** — Unresolved debt cards are negative VP at game end.

#### Attribute Thresholds
- **Faith bonuses** — Reaching faith milestones grants King's Favor cards, VP, and special abilities.
- **Influence bonuses** — Reaching influence milestones grants access to powerful commission actions and VP.
- **Strength bonuses** — Reaching strength milestones grants combat advantages and VP.
- **Track competition** — Other players advancing on the same track creates implicit competition.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Virtue and Debt System
- **Virtue track** — Tracks your moral standing from sinful to saintly.
- **Virtuous actions** — Absolving debts, converting, and charitable acts move you toward virtue.
- **Sinful actions** — Hiring criminals, taking shortcuts, and aggressive tactics generate debt and move you toward sin.
- **Track scoring** — Your position on the virtue track affects end-game scoring. Both extremes can score well, but the middle scores poorly.

#### Paladin Abilities
- **12 unique paladins** — Each provides a different round-long ability.
- **Draft mechanism** — Paladins are drafted or dealt each round. Having the right paladin at the right time is valuable.
- **One per round** — Each paladin is used once and discarded.

#### King's Orders
- **Secret objectives** — Each player has hidden end-game scoring cards.
- **Diverse goals** — Orders may require specific attribute levels, townsfolk combinations, or fortification achievements.
- **VP swing** — Completing King's Orders can be worth significant VP.

#### Suspicion
- **Hiring dark workers** — Recruiting certain worker types or taking criminal actions raises suspicion.
- **Consequences** — High suspicion can trigger negative events or reduce VP.

#### Tavern
- **Worker storage** — Your tavern on the player board holds workers between rounds.
- **Capacity limit** — You can only hold so many workers. Excess must be used or lost.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
- **After round 7** — The game ends after the 7th round.

#### Final Scoring
- **Commissioned townsfolk** — VP printed on each townsfolk card in your tableau.
- **Fortification tiles** — VP from wall tiles placed.
- **Defeated outsiders** — VP from outsiders defeated during the game.
- **Attribute tracks** — VP based on final positions on Faith, Influence, and Strength.
- **King's Orders** — VP from completed secret objective cards.
- **King's Favor** — VP from favor cards earned.
- **Garrison workers** — VP for workers placed in your garrison.
- **Virtue track** — VP based on virtue position (high virtue and low virtue both score; middle scores least).
- **Debt penalties** — Negative VP for unresolved debt cards.
- **Provisions/silver** — Small VP for remaining resources.

#### Winner
- **Highest VP** — Player with the most victory points wins.
- **Tiebreaker** — Player with higher total attribute track positions.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Worker color is strategy** — The color of workers you recruit determines your attribute development. Plan your colors.
- **Don't ignore debt** — Unresolved debt cards are harsh penalties. Balance aggressive play with absolution.
- **Advance at least 2 attributes** — Threshold bonuses are valuable. Spreading across 2 attributes is usually better than maxing 1.
- **Paladins guide your round** — Look at your paladin's ability and plan that round's actions around it.
- **King's Orders matter** — Read your secret objectives early and work toward them throughout the game.
- **Recruit early** — Having more workers gives more actions. Prioritize building your worker pool in the first 2-3 rounds.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
#### Strategy 1: Faith Focus
- **Goal** — Maximize Faith attribute for religious bonuses and absolution efficiency.
- **Key workers** — Blue workers.
- **Key actions** — Convert, absolve, faith-based townsfolk.
- **Benefit** — King's Favor cards and high virtue scoring.

#### Strategy 2: Military Dominance
- **Goal** — Maximize Strength through attacking and fortifying.
- **Key workers** — Red workers.
- **Key actions** — Attack outsiders, fortify walls.
- **Benefit** — Direct VP from combat and fortification, plus Strength track bonuses.

#### Strategy 3: Political Influence
- **Goal** — Maximize Influence for commissioning power and political VP.
- **Key workers** — Green workers.
- **Key actions** — Commission townsfolk, influence-based actions.
- **Benefit** — Townsfolk abilities compound, and Influence track provides access to premium commissions.

#### General Principles
- **Tempo** — 7 rounds is not many. Every worker placement should advance multiple objectives.
- **Paladin drafting** — Prioritize paladins that synergize with your current needs.
- **Virtue commitment** — Decide early whether to pursue high virtue (saintly) or low virtue (sinful). The middle is worst.
